title: AI Customer Support for Solopreneurs in 2026: Intercom vs Crisp vs HelpScout vs Tidio
description: Tidio starts at $24/mo with AI chat. Drift targets enterprise. HelpScout pricing unavailable. Intercom sells audio equipment. Here's what works for solo teams.
tldr: Of the five platforms researched, only Tidio and Drift returned functional vendor pages. Tidio offers AI chat + help desk from $24.17/mo (Starter) to $749/mo (Plus), with clear limits on billable conversations. Drift targets enterprise buyers with live chat and AI agents but lists no public pricing. Intercom's domain resolves to a Korean audio equipment retailer. Crisp and HelpScout domains returned no usable product or pricing data.

body.text: Solopreneurs need customer support tools that scale without enterprise complexity. We set out to compare Intercom, Crisp, HelpScout, Tidio, and Drift — five platforms frequently cited in 2026 support-tool discussions. The research surfaced a sharp divide: two vendors publish clear pricing and AI-first positioning, while three returned either unrelated content or no data. If you're choosing AI support software solo, your real options narrow fast.

We scraped vendor homepages and pricing pages for each platform on 2026-05-25. We extracted plan tiers, feature lists, AI capabilities, and published prices. No fabricated testing — all claims trace to vendor-published material or public absence of data. When a domain returned unrelated content or no pricing, we note that explicitly.

body.name: Tidio
body.price: Starter $24.17/mo (100 billable conversations), Growth from $49.17/mo (250+ conversations), Plus from $749/mo (custom conversations)
pros: Clear per-conversation pricing with monthly and annual options (annual = 2 months free)
Lyro AI Agent handles up to 1,000 AI conversations/mo on higher tiers; 50 one-off AI conversations on Starter
Live chat, ticketing, Flows automation, and analytics in one platform
7-day free trial on Starter and Growth; no credit card required
cons: Billable-conversation model can get expensive fast if volume spikes — 2,000+ conversations require custom pricing
Starter limits Flows automation to 100 visitors reached/mo; Growth starts at 2,000
Advanced features (departments, multiproject, custom branding) locked behind Plus ($749/mo minimum)
No public data on integration depth or API limits

body.text: Tidio's pricing page lists four tiers. Starter ($24.17/mo, billed annually) includes 100 billable conversations, 50 one-off Lyro AI conversations, live chat, ticketing, and basic analytics. Growth starts at $49.17/mo for 250 billable conversations and adds advanced analytics, user permissions, macros, and automatic assignment. Plus ($749/mo) unlocks departments, multiproject workspaces, custom branding, ticketing automations, and a dedicated success manager. Premium (contact for pricing) adds guaranteed AI deployment and custom solutions. Per the vendor's pricing calculator, Lyro AI conversations scale from 0 (Starter) to 1,000+ (Premium). Flows visitor reach scales from 100 (Starter) to 100,000+ (Premium). The per-conversation model is transparent but can balloon costs if ticket volume exceeds plan limits.

body.name: Drift
pros: AI Chat agent deanonymizes site visitors and routes qualified leads into seller workflows
Real-time buyer intent scoring and account identification (company name, location, history)
Fastlane feature qualifies high-value buyers instantly, skipping form queues
ROI reporting tracks engagement metrics and attributes pipeline/revenue to chat conversations
cons: No public pricing — enterprise-only, likely four-figure monthly minimums
Positioned for B2B sales teams, not solopreneurs — feature set assumes SDR/AE org structure
Live chat and AI agent bundled with Salesloft platform; unclear if Drift standalone exists
Setup complexity: account-based routing, Rhythm workflows, and CRM integrations require technical lift

body.text: Drift's homepage emphasizes AI Chat agents that engage visitors with personalized conversations, deanonymize high-intent accounts, and route qualified leads into Salesloft's Rhythm workflows. Features include live chat, Fastlane (instant qualification via tech-stack data), and ROI reporting that attributes pipeline and revenue to chat. The vendor lists a 4.5 G2 rating and markets to sales development, account executives, and revenue operations teams. No pricing appears on the site — all paths lead to 'Talk to Sales.' The feature set and language (SDR workflows, Cadence, Forecast) signal enterprise B2B, not solo support use cases.

body.name: Intercom
body.price: Domain returns Korean audio-equipment retailer; no support-software data available
cons: Vendor domain (interm.com) resolves to an unrelated business — a Korean industrial audio supplier selling speakers, amplifiers, and fire-alarm systems
No support-software product pages, pricing, or features discoverable
Unclear if Intercom customer-support platform exists at a different domain or ceased operations

body.text: The domain provided (interm.com) returned a Korean-language e-commerce site for 인터엠 (Intercom), an audio equipment vendor. Product listings include ceiling speakers, column speakers, wall-mount speakers, amplifiers, and fire-alarm broadcast systems. Prices range from ₩3,000 to ₩4,500,000. Business registration number 106-50-89370. No customer-support software, live chat, or AI agent features appear anywhere on the site. If a customer-support platform named Intercom operates under a different domain, it was not discoverable from the research provided.

body.name: Crisp
body.price: Unknown; vendor page returned text-editor software
cons: Vendor domain (crisp.com) resolves to CRiSP Programmers Editor — a text editor for Unix, Linux, Windows, and Mac
No customer-support software or live-chat features listed
Product is a BRIEF-compatible code editor with macro language, not a support tool

body.text: The domain provided (crisp.com) returned a product page for CRiSP Programmers Editor, a text editor with BRIEF emulation for developers. Features include 100% BRIEF keyboard emulation, a C/Lisp macro language, and workstation/networked licensing. The vendor offers an annual maintenance program and academic licensing. No mention of customer support, live chat, ticketing, or AI agents. If a customer-support platform named Crisp operates elsewhere, it was not accessible from the research bundle.

body.name: HelpScout
body.price: Unknown; no vendor page data available
cons: No vendor page, pricing, or feature data returned in research
Cannot assess product capabilities, AI features, or cost structure

body.text: The research bundle included no page content or data for HelpScout. Pricing, features, and AI capabilities are unknown. For a complete evaluation, contact the vendor directly or search for current product information.

items: If you're a solopreneur handling <250 conversations/month and want AI chat + help desk in one tool: Tidio Starter ($24.17/mo annual, 7-day trial) is the only researched option with transparent pricing and AI features.

If you're scaling past 400 conversations/month and need automation, user permissions, and macros: Tidio Growth (from $49.17/mo) adds advanced analytics and automatic assignment without enterprise minimums.
If you're a B2B team with SDRs, account-based workflows, and budget for custom deployment: Drift offers intent-based routing and ROI attribution, but expect enterprise pricing (contact sales).

If you need a support tool and the research returned unrelated products (Intercom audio equipment, Crisp text editor): search independently for current vendor domains or consider alternatives like Zendesk, Freshdesk, or Front.

Drift for solopreneurs — the feature set (Fastlane, Rhythm workflows, Salesloft integration) assumes a multi-person sales org. No public pricing means you'll waste time on discovery calls only to hit enterprise minimums.
Any platform without published pricing if you're budget-constrained — opaque pricing correlates with high contract minimums and annual commitments. For solo buyers, 'contact sales' is a red flag.
Tidio Plus ($749/mo) unless you need departments, multiproject workspaces, or dedicated CSM support — Growth ($49.17/mo) covers automation, analytics, and 250+ conversations for most solo use cases.
